incident_ai_summary: Likely real attack based on the combination of RustDesk remote access tool sideloaded via unofficial installer (com.universal.manager), classified as Trojan.FakeApp/10962, with Google Play Protect disabled. The sideloaded RustDesk app (com.carriez.flutter_hbb) from an unsigned/untrusted developer enables remote screen control. Disabling Play Protect allowed the malicious APK to bypass security vetting. While RustDesk itself can be legitimate, the sideloaded deployment via non-standard installer combined with disabled security controls indicates potential device takeover preparation.
incident_name: RustDesk Remote Access Trojan with Security Disabled

incident_ai_summary: This is a real attack involving a confirmed trojan (Trojan.FakeApp/10962) deployed on a compromised device. The RustDesk remote access app with file hash fbebdd100c3e41ce75aae5a650d95ea9 was sideloaded via an untrusted installer (com.universal.manager) on a rooted device (Magisk detected) with Google Play Protect disabled. This attack chain shows deliberate defense evasion (rooting, disabling security) followed by deployment of remote access malware, indicating device takeover for potential data theft or surveillance.
incident_name: Remote Access Trojan on Rooted Device
